summary: Gruber announces a codeless language module he created for BBEdit 8 that provides syntax coloring for Apache configuration files. BBEdit 8's major new feature is support for codeless language modules via XML property list files, replacing the previous requirement for compiled C/C++ plug-ins. The module highlights comments and Apache server directives for .conf and .htaccess files. He iteratively updated it based on community feedback, eventually building a comprehensive keyword list covering all Apache 1.3 and 2.0 server directives.
key_points: BBEdit 8 introduced codeless language modules as XML property lists, replacing the need for compiled C/C++ plug-ins
Gruber created an Apache configuration syntax coloring module as a practical demonstration of this new capability
The module was developed during the beta period, not hastily after release
Community feedback drove multiple updates, expanding from an incomplete keyword list to a comprehensive superset of all Apache 1.3 and 2.0 directives
money_quotes: Creating codeless language modules is a cinch — each module is an XML property list file.
I was a beta-tester for BBEdit 8; I put this together a few weeks ago, not in the hour since BBEdit 8 was publicly released.
key_insight: BBEdit 8's shift to codeless language modules via simple XML property lists dramatically lowered the barrier to extending the editor's syntax support.
